title: Diasperus blanchetianus

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its numerous health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus blanchetianus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
